items.title: Junior Geotechnical Field Engineer
items.summary: For early-career engineers who want to support offshore, nearshore, and onshore geotechnical field execution.
items.imageAlt: Offshore geotechnical operation with AG Geodrill
items.overview: This dummy opening represents a project-based technical role supporting drilling, CPT, sampling, field records, and daily coordination under senior supervision.
responsibilities: Assist field engineers during geotechnical investigation activities.
Maintain clear field notes, sample records, and daily progress updates.
Follow method statements, QHSE controls, and site communication routines.
requirements: Diploma or bachelor background in civil, geology, geotechnical, or related engineering.
Fresh graduate with field exposure or up to two years of relevant experience.
Willing to work on project sites and follow field safety discipline.

items.title: Survey Data Assistant
items.summary: For fresh graduates interested in survey data checking, positioning records, bathymetry support, and acquisition documentation.
items.overview: This dummy opening is prepared for candidates who enjoy technical software, structured records, and the accuracy required in geophysical survey workflows.
responsibilities: Support survey data organization and quality checking.
Assist positioning, bathymetry, and acquisition documentation workflows.
Prepare clean handover notes for processing and reporting teams.
requirements: Fresh graduate from geophysics, hydrography, oceanography, surveying, or related fields.
Comfortable using computers, maps, spreadsheets, and technical records.
Detail-oriented and willing to learn onboard and office workflows.

items.title: Internship Program
items.summary: For active students who want to learn from THI departments and understand how technical, field, and support work connects.
items.overview: Internship placement depends on department readiness and project needs. Profiles may be reviewed for technical, QHSE, commercial, finance, HR, GA, or marketing support.
responsibilities: Support assigned department tasks under supervision.
Learn documentation, coordination, and project support routines.
Prepare simple reports or learning outputs as required.
requirements: Active student from a relevant technical or business support major.
Able to follow instructions and communicate progress clearly.
Interested in learning from real project and company operations.
